enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request asking if he likes pancakes. He says he does enjoy them but rarely makes them since he typically eats oatmeal, canned fruit, and protein powder for breakfast. He prefers waffles over pancakes for ease of cooking, and mentions pancake mix is great for backpacking trips due to its light weight and high calorie density, drawing on his experience traveling Europe in 2010.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Likes them but rarely eats them due to his usual breakfast routine
Prefers waffles because they require less effort than flipping pancakes
Considers it an excellent backpacking food due to light weight and high calorie density
Believes bread and grain are the most effective foods for energy while traveling

transcript.text: Hello, hello. Here's a video request by my homie, Mark Del Rio.
What's up, Kyle? Next video request, five months ago.
But I did it, even though it was five months later.
Do you like pancakes? If so, what do you think about it? Please tell me your thoughts.
Okay. I like pancakes, yes. Pancakes are good. I don't eat pancakes very often.
Um, because they take, I eat, I eat, uh, um, oatmeal and, uh, canned fruit and, um, powdered, um, protein powder for breakfast.
Um, but I like pancakes. Um, pancakes are good to make. I should make more of them.
I get, I got to get, hmm, crusty ass types of pancakes that, um, we just add the water and then you throw them.
Um, I actually, I don't do pancakes. I do waffles. Waffles are better than pancakes because you just throw it in.
You make the mix and then you just pour it in. With a pancake, you have to kind of flip it around.
It's, it's more work, you know, with waffles, it just turns green and pick it out.
Yeah. And then you can do your, um, your, um, um, syrup and your, uh, your little bit of butter and it's good, healthy, you know,
But I don't do it that much, but yeah, they're good. Pancakes are definitely, definitely good, good, good food.
And also, you know, for traveling, you know, like the Oregon Trail people, um, it's a good lightweight to, to travel.
Like if you're, if you're, uh, on a backpacking trip, it would be a good way because, uh, it's just grain, you know,
And grain doesn't weigh much and it's, so it's, it's a lot of calories for how much weight you're bringing and, uh, you know,
Water comes along out the rivers and the streams and so, yeah, you just get, bring it there and, and it puffs up in this nice tasty thing.
And, uh, grain, um, you know, bread and grain is a good, a lot of calories, a good way to give you, uh, food and energy to move down the road.
Uh, when I was back touring in Europe in 2010, I remember eating, like, vegetables and fruit and I was like,
It doesn't really do it for you. What does it does it for you is bread, you know, bread and grain and pancakes,
So good energy, good breakfast food.
Okay, um, peace out.
Oops.
